what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment linked to a design-source channel memory entry.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
It declares an attachment_type of images.
Its target is the URI image://design-source/vt-residential/p-1.
It belongs to the design-source channel.
It records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format.
standouts: The target uses an image:// URI scheme scoped to design-source/vt-residential/p-1.
The file is named att-a158c67d.md matching its id.
It is a typed attachment record rather than a narrative document.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
References an external asset via a channel-scoped URI.
Keeps the record minimal with no descriptive body.
concerns: No caption, title, or alt text describes the image content.
No added_by or provenance field identifies who created/attached it.
The image:// URI cannot be resolved or validated from this file alone.
No versioning or checksum is recorded for the referenced asset.
notes: YAML-only attachment metadata card that links the design-source channel to a specific residential image asset.
questions: What does the image at vt-residential/p-1 actually depict?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is the image:// URI resolvable and versioned elsewhere?
How does this attachment relate to other design-source channel entries?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is an attachment metadata record; its content matches that stated purpose.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to register an image asset as a retrievable attachment under the design-source channel memory.
novelty: It uses an image:// URI scheme scoped to the channel to reference the actual asset.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed pointer that connects a channel memory entry to a specific image asset.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record (id att-a158c67d) in the design-source channel that declares attachment_type images and targets image://design-source/vt-residential/p-1 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to catalog and link an image asset to a design-source channel memory entry.
